articles.title: Memory
articles.author: Kate Richards
articles.complete_text:
They say
That time erases
Absent faces
From the memory
Of the heart;
That always
Years will dry
The tears,
Cleanse
The memory's
Bitter part.

There must
Be some;
Perhaps but one
Lone place
Where nature slips;
For still
At night
I waken with
Your name
Upon my lips.
articles.notes: Complete standalone poem with clear title and author attribution

articles.title: Desert Artist
articles.author: Ida Isaacson
articles.complete_text:
Under Arizona's desert sky,
With scarce save God to hear her sigh,
She works upon her Navajo
And weaves a tale of long ago.

Before her loom, erect and proud,
Aloof as over-hanging cloud,
She works upon her Navajo
And weaves this tale of long ago.

The day is long, the air is dry,
Just picot blue in cloud-choked sky
To ease the pressure of her view
While working on this pattern true,
This gray and white and red design
That she must keep in perfect line.
articles.notes: Complete standalone poem about a Navajo weaver with clear title and author

articles.title: There Is No Sound
articles.author: LeRoy Burke Meagher
articles.complete_text:
I am lonely.
When autumn's burning
Is a mound of ash
White upon the hill;
No sound or slash
Of wings in colored symphony;
In the leafless vine
No cricket shrill;
But this is mine—
The certainty
Of the slow, sure turning
Of a crocus.
articles.notes: Complete standalone poem with clear title and author attribution

articles.title: Reminder
articles.author: Maryhale Woolsey
articles.complete_text:
I've missed small Babsie—
That lovable bright elf
Who seemed a sweeter, daintier
Replica of myself

Babs in rosebud pinafore
Stained with mud-pie batter
Angel-sweet in ruffled nightie,
Imp-full of bedtime chatter!

Barbara, eighteen, and slim and tall,
Is princess-lovely, quiet, woman-wise;
How startling, now and then, to meet a smile
From roguish Babsie, out of Barbara's eyes.
articles.notes: Complete standalone poem about a mother reflecting on her daughter growing up

articles.title: Paeans
articles.author: Pansye H. Powell
articles.complete_text:
How good is our life, just for the living,
As weeping or smiling we go.
Forever advancing, the strong heart
Hears life in the warm, pulsing flow
Of music, though wordless, yet singing
Great songs of the heart that employ
Oboe and trumpet and tuba
In paeans of courage and joy!
articles.notes: Complete standalone poem celebrating life with clear title and author

misc_summary: Contains story continuation from 'Hour of Decision', advertisements for Christmas cantatas and books, music company ads, reader letters section 'From Near and Far', and various commercial advertisements for binding services and chocolates.
